key_sections.rate_or_rule: In cases of regular employment, the employer shall not terminate the services of an employee except for a just cause or when authorized by the Labor Code.
key_sections.notes: Articles renumbered pursuant to DOLE Department Advisory No. 01, Series of 2015. Old numbering in brackets.

key_sections.rate_or_rule: Just causes: (a) serious misconduct or willful disobedience; (b) gross and habitual neglect; (c) fraud or willful breach of trust; (d) commission of a crime or offense against the employer or family; (e) analogous causes.
key_sections.notes: Requires twin-notice procedure: first notice specifying grounds, opportunity to be heard, then second notice of termination decision.

key_sections.topic: Closure of establishment and reduction of personnel — authorized causes
key_sections.rate_or_rule: Authorized causes: installation of labor-saving devices; redundancy; retrenchment to prevent losses; closure or cessation of operations; disease. Employee entitled to one month pay or one-half month pay per year of service, whichever is higher, except closure due to serious losses.
key_sections.notes: Employer must serve written notice to employee and DOLE at least one month before intended date of termination.

key_sections.rate_or_rule: Permits legitimate contracting and subcontracting. Where labor-only contracting is found, the principal is deemed the direct employer of the contractor's employees.
key_sections.notes: Implemented by DOLE Department Order No. 174-17.

key_sections.rate_or_rule: All employers are required to pay all their rank-and-file employees a 13th month pay not later than December 24 of every year. (Amended by Memorandum Order No. 28, 1986 to apply to all rank-and-file employees, removing the original ₱1,000 salary cap.)
key_sections.notes: 13th month pay = 1/12 of total basic salary earned by employee within the calendar year. Excludes overtime pay, allowances, and other monetary benefits.

key_sections.rate_or_rule: Employers already paying their employees a 13th month pay or its equivalent are not covered.
key_sections.notes: Managerial employees are excluded from coverage under DOLE Implementing Rules.

key_sections.topic: Employer and employee contribution rates and schedule
key_sections.rate_or_rule: Total contribution rate: 15% of Monthly Salary Credit (MSC). Employer share: 10%; Employee share: 5%. Effective January 2025 (final scheduled increase under RA 11199). MSC range: ₱5,000 minimum to ₱35,000 maximum.
key_sections.notes: Rate increased in stages from 11% (2019) to 14% (2023) to 15% (2025) per the schedule under RA 11199. Employer also pays Employees' Compensation (EC) contributions separately.

key_sections.rate_or_rule: Premium contribution rate: 5% of monthly basic salary. Employer share: 2.5%; Employee share: 2.5%. Monthly salary floor: ₱10,000; Monthly salary ceiling: ₱100,000. Minimum monthly premium: ₱500; Maximum: ₱5,000.
key_sections.notes: 5% is the final rate under the UHC Law's phased schedule. PhilHealth confirmed no further increase for 2026.

statutes.statute_name: Home Development Mutual Fund Law of 2009

key_sections.rate_or_rule: 2% of monthly compensation (employee); 2% of monthly compensation (employer). Maximum Fund Salary (MFS): ₱10,000 per month (effective February 2024 per HDMF Circular No. 460). Maximum monthly contribution per party: ₱200.
key_sections.notes: MFS increased from ₱5,000 to ₱10,000 by HDMF Circular No. 460 (issued 15 January 2024). For members with monthly compensation of ₱1,500 or below, employee rate is 1% and employer rate is 2%.

key_sections.rate_or_rule: Establishes Regional Tripartite Wages and Productivity Boards (RTWPBs) in each region of the Philippines to prescribe minimum wages in their respective regions.
key_sections.notes: Minimum wages are region-specific and industry-specific. RTWPBs issue regional wage orders. The National Capital Region (NCR) minimum wage for non-agricultural sector is set by the NCR RTWPB.

statutes.statute_name: DOLE Department Order No. 174, Series of 2017

key_sections.rate_or_rule: A contracting arrangement is legitimate if: (a) the contractor carries on a distinct and independent business; (b) the contractor has substantial capital (₱5 million for corporations, partnerships, cooperatives; ₱5 million net worth for single proprietors); (c) the service agreement ensures compliance with labor standards; (d) the contractor does not engage in labor-only contracting.
key_sections.notes: Issued under Articles 106-109 of the Labor Code. Replaced DOLE Department Order No. 18-A. Prohibits 'labor-only contracting' and other illicit contractual arrangements including the 'endo' scheme.

key_sections.topic: Criteria for lawful processing of personal information
key_sections.rate_or_rule: Personal information may be processed only when consent is given; when processing is necessary for a contract; when required by law; to protect vitally important interests; when necessary for public functions; or when necessary for legitimate interests.
key_sections.notes: Administered by the National Privacy Commission. Relevant to employment records and data handling by foreign employers of Philippine-based remote staff.
